task_completion.description: Verify the agent selects or targets the 'acmecorp.test' virtual server.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'acmecorp.test' selected in the Virtualmin domain dropdown menu, or the agent opening a configuration file specifically named after the domain (e.g., /etc/php/*/fpm/pool.d/acmecorp.test.conf or similar) in a terminal/text editor.

task_completion.description: Verify the process manager (pm) mode is set to dynamic.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'pm = dynamic' typed in a configuration file, or a 'Process Manager'/'PM' setting changed to 'Dynamic' in the Virtualmin GUI.
Look for 'pm.max_children = 40' in the configuration file, or the value '40' entered into the 'Maximum child processes' (or similar) field in the GUI.
Look for 'pm.start_servers = 10' in the configuration file, or the value '10' entered into the 'Initial child processes' (or similar) field in the GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify pm.min_spare_servers is set to 5 and pm.max_spare_servers is set to 15.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for 'pm.min_spare_servers = 5' and 'pm.max_spare_servers = 15' in the configuration file, or the values '5' and '15' entered into the minimum and maximum idle/spare processes fields in the GUI.

task_completion.description: Verify the configuration changes are saved and applied.
task_completion.visual_evidence: Look for the agent clicking a 'Save' or 'Apply' button in the Virtualmin GUI, followed by a success message, OR the agent saving the file in a text editor and running a command to restart/reload the PHP-FPM service (e.g., 'systemctl restart php8.1-fpm' or similar).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent is modifying the actual PHP-FPM configuration via Virtualmin or a legitimate text editor/terminal.
integrity.visual_evidence: The agent should be interacting with the Virtualmin control panel (Services -> PHP-FPM Configuration) or a terminal editor (nano, vim) editing a .conf file. It should not be typing the answers into a blank text document or a mock interface.

integrity.description: Ensure the changes are applied to the specific domain pool, not globally.
integrity.visual_evidence: The configuration screen or file path must clearly indicate it belongs to 'acmecorp.test' (e.g., the pool name at the top of the file should be [acmecorp.test] or the Virtualmin context must be set to this domain).

integrity.description: Ensure the agent did not use browser developer tools to fake the configuration values.
integrity.visual_evidence: There should be no browser developer tools (Elements/Console tab) open to manipulate the HTML of the Virtualmin page to display the target values.

privileged_info_for_vlm: Target domain: 'acmecorp.test'. The task requires modifying the PHP-FPM pool configuration for this domain. The required values are: pm = dynamic, pm.max_children = 40, pm.start_servers = 10, pm.min_spare_servers = 5, and pm.max_spare_servers = 15. These can be set via the Virtualmin GUI or by editing the domain's pool configuration file (typically located in /etc/php/*/fpm/pool.d/). The configuration must be saved and the PHP-FPM service must be restarted or reloaded for the changes to apply.
